page: UpCloud 16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores on an AMD EPYC 7543 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 1280 GB local SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The underlying x86_64 architecture supports several instruction set extensions, including AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, which accelerate cryptographic and vector calculations. However, it lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions. The CPU features a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% of evaluated instances. Multi-core CPU performance falls within the middle 50% average, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 47789.69 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11156.0. Memory bandwidth write tests for large uncached blocks also show strong performance in the top 25% at 96.49 GB/sec, while read-only memory bandwidth remains average.

This resource configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations. The instance achieved an average of 29.54 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 15.34 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. Given the shared CPU allocation, it offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring substantial memory and storage capacity without requiring dedicated, constant CPU execution.
description: UpCloud 16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose server instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC 7543 processor. It features 16 shared vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1280 GB local SSD. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions, though it lacks AVX-512. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance and strong large-block memory write bandwidth, both ranking in the top 25%. Other metrics, including multi-core CPU performance, memory read bandwidth, and database operations, align with the middle 50% average. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local SSD storage, this instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that benefit from a qualitative balance of compute, memory, and storage resources.
og_description: UpCloud 16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 1280 GB local SSD, delivering strong single-core performance and balanced resources.
meta_description: UpCloud 16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose KVM server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a bundled 1280 GB local SSD.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring sixteen shared cores, sixty-four gigabytes of memory, and a generous bundled local SSD.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC 7543 vCPUs and 64 GB of system memory
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory write performance
Supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI hardware acceleration
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
